name: Narayanakantha
dates_notes: Placed in tenth-century Kashmir as a Sanskrit Śaiva Siddhānta commentator and father of Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha II.
core_thesis: Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha is a tenth-century Kashmirian Śaiva Siddhānta commentator associated with the *Mṛgendravṛtti*, a commentary on the *Mṛgendrāgama*. His importance for the timeline is genealogical and textual: he belongs to the Sanskrit Saiddhāntika commentarial environment that precedes Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha II and preserves the dualist Siddhānta attention to scripture, ritual order, and the distinct realities of Lord, soul, and bond. The local OCR is a mixed Sanskrit/French edition scan, so this entry does not make fine-grained doctrinal claims beyond the attested work and school placement.

engaged_works.title: Mrgendra-vrtti
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Standardly attributed to Bhaṭṭa Nārāyaṇakaṇṭha in the Śaiva Siddhānta edition and secondary literature.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on the *Mṛgendrāgama*, one of the Śaiva Siddhānta scriptural sources. The work comments on Saiddhāntika doctrine and ritual in the same broad tradition that later Kashmirian authors systematize more polemically. Its value here is as a witness to the scriptural-commentarial setting of Kashmir Śaiva Siddhānta before Bhaṭṭa Rāmakaṇṭha II. The local corpus contains Internet Archive OCR of a Sanskrit/French edition scan.
